items.headline: Jason Robertson, Stars honor veteran with trip to Game 5
items.description:
DALLAS -- Jason Robertson hosts a family of an active-duty service member at every Dallas Stars home game throughout the regular season. The program is called "JR's Heroes" and it's been ongoing since the start of the 2021-22 season.
"I don't think I've ever done it in the playoffs, honestly,"

items.headline: Coaches Room: Pittsburgh Penguins ride momentum into Game 6 against Flyers
items.description:
The Coaches Room is a regular feature throughout the 2025-26 season by former NHL coaches and assistants who turn their critical gaze to the game and explain it through the lens of a teacher.
In this edition, Dan Bylsma, former coach of the Pittsburgh Penguins, Buffalo Sabres and Seattle Kraken

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres fans sing O Canada before Game 5
items.description:
No audio, no problem in Buffalo.
Fans at KeyBank Center stepped up during the singing of “O Canada” before Game 5 of the Eastern Conference First Round on Tuesday after anthem singer Cami Clune’s microphone cut out.
Once the crowd realized what was going on, fans didn’t miss a beat

items.headline: Mats Zuccarello Yakov Trenin injury status for Minnesota for Game 5
items.description:
DALLAS -- Mats Zuccarello and Yakov Trenin returned for the Minnesota Wild in Game 5 of the Western Conference First Round against the Dallas Stars at American Airlines Center on Tuesday.
Zuccarello had missed the past three games and Trenin the past two, each with an upper-body injury.

items.headline: Tampa Bay Lightning Victor Hedman playing status update April 28 2026
items.description:
BRANDON, Fla. -- Victor Hedman couldn’t stop smiling Tuesday.
Neither could many of his Tampa Bay Lightning teammates.

items.headline: Juraj Slafkovsky to play for Montreal Canadiens in Game 5
items.description:
BROSSARD, Quebec -- Juraj Slafkovsky did not practice with the Montreal Canadiens on Tuesday but will be in the lineup for Game 5 of the Eastern Conference First Round at the Tampa Bay Lightning on Wednesday (7 p.m. ET; TheSpot, ESPN2, SN, TVAS, CBC).

items.headline: Pittsburgh Penguins core has belief team can complete comeback vs Philadelphia Flyers
items.description:
CRANBERRY, Pa. -- Sidney Crosby, Evgeni Malkin and Kris Letang have nearly seen it all.
In their 20 seasons together with the Pittsburgh Penguins, they’ve won the Stanley Cup three times (2009, 2016, 2017). Crosby, in 2016 and 2017, and Malkin, in 2009, have won the Conn Smythe Trophy as

items.headline: Utah coach Andre Tourigny 'real student of the game'
items.description:
SANDY, Utah -- Don't judge a Bear by its cover.
Andre Tourigny is nicknamed "Bear" not because he's a big, burly guy who can growl. It's because he once bear-hugged someone, and everyone started calling him "Bear," which is easier for English speakers to pronounce than his French last name.

items.headline: Philadelphia Flyers still in a good spot entering Game 6 against Pittsburgh Penguins
items.description:
If someone had told Sean Couturier before the Eastern Conference First Round series against the Pittsburgh Penguins began that the Philadelphia Flyers would be coming home with a 3-2 series lead, he'd have taken it.
Despite losing two straight games after taking a 3-0 series lead, that's where the Flyers

items.headline: Nikolaj Ehlers Alexander Nikishin injury status update April 28 2026 Carolina Hurricanes playoffs
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. -- Nikolaj Ehlers and Alexander Nikishin could be available for the Carolina Hurricanes at the start of the Eastern Conference Second Round.
“I’m hopeful that both guys will be ready by the looks of it,” Hurricanes coach Rod Brind’Amour said after neither practiced Tuesday.

items.headline: Connor McDavid injury status update April 28 2026 Edmonton Oilers Game 5 vs Anaheim Ducks
items.description: Connor McDavid and Jason Dickinson will each be a game-time decision for the Edmonton Oilers in Game 5 of the Western Conference First Round against the Anaheim Ducks at Rogers Place in Edmonton on Tuesday (10 p.m. ET; CBC, TVAS, SN, KCOP-13, TNT, truTV, Victory+, HBO MAX).

items.headline: Energy level is there for Edmonton to rally in West 1st Round
items.description:
EDMONTON -- Kris Knoblauch believes the Edmonton Oilers have the drive to mount a comeback against the Anaheim Ducks in the Western Conference First Round.
